h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to place force labels on the cart diagram based on physical contact.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [puts the contact strip under the rightward arrow and moves the rolling-left strip into the side space] "Under the arrow: hand on the cart, pushing right. The rolling-left sentence goes over here, because it says what the cart does, not what is acting on it. The two can sit apart, though the left-roll case still feels a bit awkward."
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the new sorting task, physically separating the interaction description from the motion description.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [slides two sentence strips below the sideways arrow, then sets a blank “not a force label” space to the side] "The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space."
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from simple card placement to a sorting task with sentence strips to address the learner's confusion about force versus motion.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: [slides two sentence strips below the sideways arrow, then sets a blank “not a force label” space to the side] "The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space."
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The sorting task is highly fitted to the learner's specific confusion, providing a clear spatial separation between force and motion concepts.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.
